MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-28-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, PLAINTIFF DAVID APPELBAUM'S MOTION TO RESCIND SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS DENIED. MR. APPLEBAUM HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THERE HAS BEEN A MATERIAL FAILURE OF CONSIDERATION. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY WAS REQUIRED TO WITHHOLD TAXES FROM THE AMOUNT PAYABLE TO MR. APPELBAUM AND REMIT THE WITHHOLDING TO THE STATE AND FEDERAL TAXING AUTHORITIES. WITHHOLDING OF TAXES WAS ALSO CONTEMPLATED BY THE PARTIES PER THE EXPRESS LANGUAGE OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. ANY ERROR BY PG&E IN THE CALCULATION OF THE AMOUNTS WITHHELD WHICH RESULTED IN OVERWITHOLDING DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A MATERIAL FAILURE OF CONSIDERATION AND MR. APPELBAUM'S REMEDY IS TO SEEK REFUNDS. (RIVERA V. BAKER WEST, INC. (9TH CIR. 2005) 430 F.3D 1253, 1260 ("RIVERA IS NOT WITHOUT RECOURSE, EITHER AS TO THE FACT OR THE AMOUNT OF INCOME TAX WITHHELD. HE REMAINS FREE TO SEEK A REFUND FOR WRONGFULLY WITHHELD TAXES VIA A DIRECT CLAIM TO THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE."); CIFUENTES V. COSTCO WHOLESALE CORP. (2015) 238 CAL.APP.4TH 65, 80 (PLAINTIFF'S "REMEDY WAS TO SEEK REFUNDS FOR ANY EXCESS WITHHOLDING, NOT FURTHER DAMAGES FROM" THE DEFENDANT)). FORMAL ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/HK)
